message:
fix(net): harden H2 RST_STREAM rate limiting, randomize DNS query IDs, fix WebSocket close validation, cancel WASM stream on drop

H2 RST_STREAM flood protection (connection.rs):
- Reset rst_stream_count to 0 (not 1) on window reset so the "N allowed,
  N+1 rejected" contract holds exactly at the configured ceiling.
- Check the count *before* incrementing to prevent u32::MAX wrapping —
  previously incrementing first would wrap to 0 and silently re-allow
  a flood that had already hit the ceiling.
- Added regression test that sets count to u32::MAX-1, confirms the
  MAX-th RST is allowed, and the (MAX+1)-th is rejected with
  EnhanceYourCalm without the counter wrapping.

DNS query ID generation (resolver.rs):
- Replace the deterministic FNV-hash-based query ID derivation with
  entropy-sourced random IDs via the EntropySource trait (OsEntropy in
  production). The old scheme derived IDs from (name, qtype, attempt,
  nameserver_index), making them predictable and vulnerable to DNS
  cache poisoning if an attacker can observe or guess query patterns.
- Remove the now-unnecessary `consumed` tracking variable from
  decode_dns_name_inner — the offset is always computed from the current
  position, making the bookkeeping dead code.
- Added tests: entropy-based ID generation, zero-remapping to 0xA5A5,
  zero-terminator consumption, and compression pointer byte consumption.

WebSocket close validation (close.rs):
- Call is_valid_received_code instead of is_valid_code when parsing
  incoming close frames, so server-only codes (e.g. 1011) are accepted
  when received from a peer.

WASM readable stream (browser_stream.rs):
- Cancel the underlying ReadableStream on drop if it hasn't been fully
  consumed (self.done == false), preventing the browser from holding the
  stream's internal queue and backpressure state indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
